name: Walporzheimer Gärkammer
description: Most famous vineyard in Ahr. Steep slate slopes produce elegant, complex Spätburgunder (Pinot Noir) with great aging potential.
historicalNotes: Name means 'fermentation chamber.' Meyer-Näkel, Jean Stodden, Kreuzberg make benchmark examples.

name: Walporzheimer Kräuterberg
description: Steep slate vineyard producing structured, mineral Spätburgunder. One of Ahr's finest sites.
historicalNotes: Name means 'herb mountain.' Meyer-Näkel and Deutzerhof make outstanding examples.

name: Dernauer Pfarrwingert
description: Very steep slopes producing elegant, perfumed Spätburgunder with finesse. Top site in Dernau.
historicalNotes: Name means 'pastor's vineyard.' Meyer-Näkel owns prime parcels. Among the steepest vineyards in Germany.

name: Mayschosser Mönchberg
description: Steep slate slopes producing mineral, elegant Spätburgunder. Classic Ahr terroir.
historicalNotes: Name means 'monk's mountain,' referring to monastic viticulture history.

name: Neuenahrer Sonnenberg
description: Warm, sunny slopes producing ripe, structured Spätburgunder. Name means 'sun mountain.'
historicalNotes: Deutzerhof and Kreuzberg make excellent examples from this well-exposed site.

name: Ahrweiler Rosenthal
description: Mixed volcanic terroir producing complex Spätburgunder with excellent depth.
historicalNotes: Historic Ahrweiler site with multiple quality producers.
